content.problemStatement:
Write a program to print the length of each word of the sentence given below as well as the length of the whole sentence.

**Note** - Make sure to give a space after every word or "-" when printing
constraints: The program works with a single predefined sentence; no runtime constraints.
content.outputFormat: Print four lines. Each of the first three lines contains a word of the sentence, a space, a hyphen, a space and the length of that word. The fourth line contains the whole sentence, a space, a hyphen, a space and the length of the complete sentence (including spaces).

sampleTestCases.explanation: The three words are "Coding" (6 letters), "on" (2 letters) and "CodeChef" (8 letters). The full sentence "Coding on CodeChef" contains 18 characters including the two spaces.

solution.approach: The sentence is fixed: "Coding on CodeChef". Store each word in a variable, compute its length using the language's built‑in length function, and build the full sentence by concatenating the words with spaces. Print each word with its length and finally the whole sentence with its length. The algorithm runs in constant time and uses constant extra space.
solution.timeComplexity: O(1) – only a few string length operations.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few fixed‑size strings.

code.Python3:
#!/usr/bin/env python3
w1 = "Coding"
w2 = "on"
w3 = "CodeChef"
sentence = f"{w1} {w2} {w3}"
T = 1  # loop to satisfy the generic requirement
for _ in range(T):
    print(f"{w1} - {len(w1)}")
    print(f"{w2} - {len(w2)}")
    print(f"{w3} - {len(w3)}")
    print(f"{sentence} - {len(sentence)}")
